title: FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources for unpaid relative caregivers. Resources include information about available services, assistance in gaining access to services, individual counseling, support groups, caregiver training, respite care, and supplemental services, on a limited basis. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals 60 years of age and older -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals of any age with Alzheimer’s disease and related disorders -Older relatives (not parents) age 55 and older, providing care to children under the age of 18 -Older relatives, including parents, age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (503) 397-3511 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 397-3511 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, or visit website for more information. Support group information is available by phone or online. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Columbia County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Services: Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups Caregiver Counseling Caregiver Training * Kinship Caregivers Caregiver/Care Receiver Support Groups Family Caregiver Subsidies Respite Care Subsidies Specialized Information and Referral * Kinship Caregivers Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING PROGRAMS HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING REHABILITATION PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES LEGAL SERVICES - VETERANS SERVICES OFFICE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - RCI REACH MONEY MANAGEMENT - MONEY MANAGEMENT PROGRAM UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM
